events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario describes a harness impact indicator deployed (webbing tag showing). This means the harness has been subjected to an impact load and must be removed from service. This is a failed/defective condition.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "failed", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 80,
  "value": 0,

  "confidence": 0.95,

  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Personal fall arrest harness impact indicator deployed (webbing tag exposed/torn), indicating the harness has arrested a fall or been shock-loaded",
    "Harness with deployed impact indicator must be removed from service; cannot be returned to service without manufacturer determination",
    "Fall protection equipment showing evidence of prior fall arrest is non-compliant for continued use under OSHA 1910.140 and 1926 Subpart M"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately remove the affected harness from service and tag as 'Do Not Use'",
    "Inspect all associated components (lanyard, SRL, anchor, connectors) for damage from the same event",
    "Replace the harness with a new, inspected, certified unit before any further elevated work",
    "Document the incident and determine whether a fall occurred; investigate root cause"
  ],
  "rationale": "A deployed impact indicator on a PFAS harness is definitive evidence the harness has been subjected to fall-arrest forces. Under OSHA 1910.140(d)(2) and 1926 Subpart M, personal fall arrest equipment that has arrested a fall must be removed from service and not used again until determined serviceable by a competent person or per manufacturer instructions. This is a critical safety failure if the harness remains in use.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1926 Subpart M - Fall Protection",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.140 - Personal Fall Protection Systems",
    "OSHA 1910.140(d)(2) - inspection and removal from service after fall arrest"
  ]
}
